title: Edit safety: read-before-write tracking + diff output in modify_file
current_state: modify_file edits blind: no requirement that the model read the current content first, no diff in the result, no protection against whole-file overwrite of unread content. This is how agents corrupt files.
target_state: Per-session read-ledger: read_file records (path, mtime). modify_file on a tracked file whose mtime changed since read → structured stale-read error telling the model to re-read. modify_file on a NEVER-read file → refusal with 'read it first'. Every successful modify_file returns a unified diff (difflib) of what changed plus line counts, so the model (and the L1 log) sees exactly what it did. create_file on an existing path → refusal pointing at modify_file.
migration_plan: 1. ExecutionTools gains self._read_ledger: dict[path → mtime] populated by read_file.
2. modify_file preflight: not in ledger → {'error':'read_before_write', hint}; mtime drift → {'error':'stale_read', hint}; ledger updates after successful write.
5. Tests: write-without-read refused; read→write ok; external modification between read and write → stale_read; diff content correct.
risk_notes: Pure tools/ change — sprint.py-eligible. Keep the ledger on the ExecutionTools instance (per-session), not global.
